note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment aims to assess gene amplification using FISH (Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization) in formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded cancer specimens. The goal is to determine the presence and extent of HER2 gene amplification.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "1. Deparaffinize samples using xylene for 10 minutes.",
    "2. Wash samples with 100% ethanol for 5 minutes.",
    "3. Rehydrate samples with 70% ethanol for 5 minutes.",
    "4. Extract proteins from the samples using 0.2 N HCl at room temperature for 20 minutes.",
    "5. Perform protein extraction again using 0.1 M hot citric acid at 95°C for 20 minutes.",
    "6. Rinse samples with 2x SSC briefly.",
    "7. Wash samples with 0.4x SSC containing 0.3% Igepal CA-630 for 5 minutes each, agitate initially.",
    "8. Perform auto-fluorescence quenching with reagents for 2 to 5 minutes.",
    "9. Stain samples with DAPI for 10 minutes.",
    "10. Rinse samples with 2x SSC for 5 minutes."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded cancer specimens",
    "Xylene",
    "100% ethanol",
    "70% ethanol",
    "0.2 N HCl",
    "0.1 M hot citric acid",
    "Igepal CA-630",
    "DAPI"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Boeckel Scientific programmable slide mat",
    "Microcentrifuge tubes",
    "Laminar flow hood
